title: Problem with monday doc column and templates

posts.author: NickySiba (Nicky Schelfaut)
posts.cookedHtml:
On every item on our board we wanted a place to write notes that are transferable to the next person in a proces. We also wanted a nice little template.

On this community I found a great solution:
-A monday doc column with a template (you can set a template with column settings)

So everytime you click in the column to create a mondaydoc, a nice little doc with template is made.

I first made a monday doc and saved that doc and added it as a template

template example656×420 29.3 KB

I then went to the colum settings and set this template as the default template for this column. Worked great…

But now I wanted to update this template because after using it for a while we wanted to add some stuff. So for new created monday docs I wanted an updated template.

So I thougt editing the template that you see in the picture above would do the trick. But no… I would still get the old template when creating a new mondaydoc.

I then found out that I could edit the template that was used for the column by going to column settings and edit the template there (’ Docsjabloon bewerken’ means edit template in dutch).

edit template1270×412 37.5 KB

If I edit the template there it will be updated for every new mondaydoc made in the column.

It’s not what I expected. It seems it copies the original set template for the column? And since I used the template on 2 boards, I now need to edit the template seperatly on 2 boards in the mondaydoc column. Instead of just editting that one template that I thought would be linked to the columns.

I asked support but they kept explaining that updating a template would not update existing docs. But that was not the point of my question, I get that. It’s about the template for new docs.

Am I missing something? Or is this just the way it is.

posts.author: Matt_Frost (Dr Matt Frost - www.frostgroup.uk)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was confused by this when I started using docs as a template too.

I THINK what happens is that when you set a monday doc as a template, it creates ANOTHER template that exists just for that column, and isn’t actually linked to the original template document anymore.

posts.author: NickySiba (Nicky Schelfaut)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah that is what I found out too…

But it is not something that’s clearly mentioned somewhere… It is a bit annoying to be honest.

But @Matt_Frost If you also experience this I think this is the intended use.
